Karim
Zein

   

Film, TV & Commercial Production  ·  London

   

Hussein Madi (1938–2024), Untitled, 2014  ·  South Lebanon

 
 

About

 
   
           

BFI London
Film Festival

   
   
     

Based in London, with roots in Beirut. Most recently completed a production assistant work experience placement at SALT.TV, an APA member production company, across commercial shoots for clients including M&G Investments and Warner Bros. Discovery (TNT Sports). Placement arranged through the Association of Production Companies.

     

The starting point was performance. Over twelve years of professional on-camera work, beginning at age eight, across the Middle East, Europe, Asia, and North America. 60 commercial campaigns, a cast role in Tom Clancy's Jack Ryan on Prime Video, and a credit in Nocturne in Black, Jimmy Keyrouz's BAFTA-winning and Oscar-shortlisted short film. That experience on sets of every scale, in every position in the room, built the instincts that now inform the work as a producer.

     

The shift from actor to producer was not a career change. It was the same drive, redirected. Understanding what makes a set work, the hierarchy, the pace, the decision-making under pressure, and moving to the side of the room where those decisions are made.

     

Beyond production, involved in new business development at SALT, agency research, outreach strategy, and company positioning. Attends APA events, reads LBB, Ad Age, and Campaign, and pays close attention to how the commercial production industry is evolving. The goal is not just to execute tasks, but to understand the industry being entered and to bring that curiosity into every room.

   
 
 

Film, TV & Commercial Production

 
   
       SALT.TV       Feb 2026 — Present    
   

Production Assistant  ·  Work Experience  ·  APA Placement  ·  London, UK

   

APA member production company. Credits include M&G Investments, Warner Bros. Discovery (TNT Sports), and Mollies (Soho House). Placement arranged through the Association of Production Companies.

   

Supported pre-production and on-set execution across commercial shoots — building call sheets, coordinating crew and talent schedules, assisting with budgeting workflows and logistics. Assisted producers with equipment coordination and technical planning across APA-standard production processes.

   

Also involved in new business development — agency research, outreach strategy, and preparation of materials to support incoming briefs and company positioning. Assisted in maintaining everyday office functions, cutting director reels, and general production admin.

 
 
   
      The FrameCode       Jun — Sep 2025    
   

Associate Producer Intern  ·  Beirut, Lebanon

   

Shadowed the lead producer across all stages of production on the McDonald's Lebanon campaign. Responsibilities included coordinating talent and crew schedules, developing call sheets, managing daily shoot logistics, and handling location permits and production approvals. A full view of how a regional commercial production operates from brief to delivery.

 
 
   
      Final Notice       2025    
   

Producer  ·  Student Short Film  ·  London, UK

   

Oversaw the production from concept development through to post. Managed budgeting, casting, scheduling, and on-set logistics across the full cycle, responsible for every operational decision from the first location recce to picture lock.

 
 
   
      Drowning       2024    
   

Producer  ·  Student Short Film  ·  London, UK

   

Coordinated production logistics, risk assessments, and compliance with safety protocols. Also responsible for the visual language of the film, the aesthetic choices, the emotional tone, and how the story was told through the frame.

 
 
  Commercial shoot on location, Premier League stadium, London   SALT.TV  ·  2026
 

Technical

 
       
  • Brief breakdowns: reading an incoming brief, identifying the key deliverables, flagging questions for the producer, and mapping out what the production will require before any paperwork begins
    •    
  • Production schedules: building day-by-day prep and shoot schedules from scratch, accounting for crew availability, location access, talent commitments, equipment delivery windows, and post timelines
    •    
  • Call sheets: compiling and distributing full call sheets including call times, location details, parking, emergency contacts, shot lists, schedule breakdowns, and department-specific notes
    •    
  • Shot lists and floor plans: working from director treatments and storyboards to prepare shot lists and floor plan references that the crew can use on the day
    •    
  • Location recces: scouting locations, identifying logistical requirements, flagging access or permit issues, and preparing location notes and risk assessments ahead of shoot
    •    
  • Risk assessments and production safety documentation: completing risk assessments in line with APA guidelines, identifying hazards, and ensuring all crew and talent are briefed on safety protocols
    •    
  • Equipment manifests: preparing kit lists in coordination with the DOP and technical team, managing hire orders, confirming delivery and collection logistics with suppliers
    •    
  • Budget tracking and cost reporting: maintaining live cost trackers across productions, reconciling actuals against estimates, flagging variances to the producer, and preparing post-production cost reports
    •    
  • Crew and talent coordination: managing availability, issuing deal memos and contracts, coordinating travel and accommodation where required, and keeping all parties informed throughout pre-production
    •    
  • APA-standard commercial production workflows across pre-production, shoot, and wrap
    •    
  • First point of contact for EPs and office operations: taking calls, managing correspondence, handling deliveries, supporting reconciliations and administrative tasks
    •    
  • Director reel cutting and post-production admin support
    •    
  • Set etiquette and full awareness of production hierarchy and chain of command
    •    
  • Client and agency-facing communication on set and in office
    •    
  • New business development: agency research, brand outreach, and materials preparation to support company positioning
    •    
  • Sony FX6, Canon C300, RED Komodo, URSA Mini Pro
    •    
  • High dynamic range and high-resolution cinema workflows
    •    
  • MS Office, Google Drive, Dropbox
    •    
  • English — Native  ·  Arabic — Native  ·  French — Elementary
    •  
 

Film, TV & Commercial Production

 
   

Tom Clancy's Jack Ryan

   

Amazon Prime Video  ·  Season 1  ·  2018  ·  Cast: Samir

   

Cast in the Amazon Prime Video series as Samir, filmed across Canada and Morocco. The production operated under showrunner Carlton Cuse and director Daniel Sackheim via Potomac Production, at the scale of a major international streaming commission.

   

Working at that level from a young age meant absorbing set etiquette, chain of command, and production culture before most people have had a single professional credit. Understanding who defers to whom, when to speak and when to stay quiet, how a set breathes when things are going well and how it tightens when they are not. That awareness is not taught. It is earned over time, on set, paying attention. It is the foundation that makes the current work as a producer feel instinctive rather than acquired.

 
   
  Left: Official poster  ·  Right: On set with Carlton Cuse (showrunner)   Prime Video  ·  S1  ·  2018
 
  On set with John Krasinski and cast  ·  Dir. Daniel Sackheim   Potomac Production  ·  2018
 
     
 

Nocturne in Black

 
   

Nocturne in Black

   

Dir. Jimmy Keyrouz  ·  2016  ·  Amazon Prime Video  ·  IMDb 7.3/10

   

Cast in Jimmy Keyrouz's short film, set in a war-ravaged Middle Eastern neighbourhood where a young pianist fights to rebuild his piano after it is destroyed by jihadists. Inspired by true events. The film went on to win the BAFTA Student Award for Live Action, the Student Academy Award Gold Medal, was shortlisted for the Oscar, and screened at Telluride, the LA Shorts Fest, and festivals across Europe and the Middle East.

   

Being part of a film at that level was an education in what a festival campaign looks like from the inside: how a short travels, how an audience is built, and how limited resources can produce something that reaches a global stage. Currently available on Amazon Prime Video.

 
   
  Left: Official poster  ·  Right: Key art with festival laurels   Dir. Jimmy Keyrouz  ·  2016
 
  Film still  ·  Nocturne in Black   Amazon Prime Video  ·  2016
 

Commercial Credits

 

Twelve years of professional on-camera experience, beginning at age eight, across the Middle East, Asia, Europe, and North America. Featured in over 60 commercial campaigns with agencies including Leo Burnett, and brands including KFC, STC, Pepsi, Nadec, and Kinder.

 

Working at that volume and across that many production cultures meant developing a genuine fluency with how sets operate: the rhythm, the hierarchy, the pace, and what is expected of everyone in the room. Comfortable with crew, comfortable with clients, comfortable with agencies. Good with people across every level of a production, which matters in a role that requires you to be front of house and in the middle of operations at the same time.

 

That background is what the producing work is built on. It is not a parallel track. It is the same track, viewed from the other side of the camera.

 

Education

 
   
      Brunel University London       2023 — 2026    
   

BA (Hons) Film Production  ·  Expected First Class

   

Three-year production degree with a focus on the producer track. Practical grounding across narrative, documentary, and commercial production workflows, alongside screen theory and critical studies. The degree ran in parallel with active industry work. The placement at SALT.TV began in the final year and has continued alongside academic completion.

   

Final project: From Sound to Code: AI and the Next Revolution in Cinema, a video essay examining how artificial intelligence is reshaping creative labour in film and commercial production, anchored in Walter Benjamin's concept of the aura.

 
 

Video Essay

 
   

From Sound to Code: AI and the Next Revolution in Cinema

   

Final year video essay  ·  Brunel University London  ·  2026

   

The central argument is that AI is not an exception to the history of technological disruption in cinema. It is a continuation of it. Synchronised sound changed performance. Television changed distribution. Streaming changed access. None of those technologies attempted to replicate the creative act itself. AI does. That distinction is the spine of the essay.

   

The theoretical framework is built around Walter Benjamin's concept of the aura, the sense of singular human presence that audiences register in a work, even when they cannot name it. The Coca-Cola AI advertisement backlash became the clearest case study: audiences identified something missing despite technically high-quality imagery. Benjamin had already named what was absent.

   

Case studies span OpenAI's Sora and the gap between technical possibility and industrial viability; the Netflix acquisition of InterPositive and what it reveals about institutional power absorbing creative tools; and the creator tools ecosystem and its false promise of democratisation. Attending the APA Future of Advertising 2026 grounded the research in live industry debate, not speculation, but the conversations happening at the highest levels of commercial production right now.

   

The conclusion is deliberately open. AI will reshape cinema. The question the essay ends on is what kind of cinema that produces: one optimised for efficiency and scale, or one that still makes space for authorship, risk, and human judgment.

    Watch above  ·  Vimeo
